title: beta vulgaris subsp. maritima
intro: Beta vulgaris subsp. maritima, also known as sea beet, is a subspecies of the beet plant that is native to the coastal areas of Europe and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural diuretic. The health benefits of sea beet include its high concentration of vitamins A and C, potassium, and iron, making it a valuable addition to a healthy diet. Sea beet can be prepared as an herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and has also been used as a natural food ingredient and dye plant.
